name: Narváez Viewpoint Trail
name_original: Parque Natural de Sierra de Baza – circular al Mirador de Narváez
description: Enjoy an easy 3.8-mile (6.2 km) hike in Sierra de Baza Natural Park, offering panoramic views from Mirador de Narváez.
region_intro: Hiking in Sierra De Baza offers a diverse range of trails within a protected natural park. The region is characterized by mountainous terrain, dense pine forests, and deep ravines. Hikers can explore varied landscapes, from high-altitude viewpoints to tranquil valleys, making it a suitable destination for different experience levels. Best hiking trails in Sierra De Baza The most popular hiking route is Narváez Viewpoint Trail, a 3.8 miles (6.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 49 minutes to complete. This easy route offers panoramic views from the Narváez Viewpoint. Another top favourite among local hikers is Barranco de la Fonfría and Pino de la Señora Trail, a difficult 11.3 miles (18.2 km) path. This trail navigates through a ravine and pine forests, showcasing the natural park's rugged beauty. Local hikers also love the Fuente de la Taza – Narváez Viewpoint loop from El Granero, a 6.6 miles (10.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain with a natural spring and a viewpoint, often completed in about 3 hours 9 minutes. Hiking in Sierra De Baza is defined by mountainous landscapes, extensive pine forests, and scenic viewpoints. The network offers options for easy family friendly walks, moderate day hikes, and more challenging routes. The routes in Sierra De Baza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Sierra De Baza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Sierra De Baza?
A: Sierra De Baza features over 40 hiking routes. This includes 20 easy trails, 17 moderate trails, and 4 more challenging difficult routes, ensuring options for all experience levels.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Sierra De Baza?
A: Yes, Sierra De Baza has several easy trails suitable for families. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981934301/narvaez-viewpoint-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Narváez Viewpoint Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.8 miles (6.2 km) long and offers panoramic views without being overly strenuous.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in Sierra De Baza?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails within Sierra De Baza Natural Park, but it's always best to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety. Please check specific trail regulations if posted, especially in sensitive areas.

Q: What are the best times of year to go hiking in Sierra De Baza?
A: The best times for hiking in Sierra De Baza are typically spring (April to June) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, and the natural landscapes are particularly vibrant. Summer can be very hot, while winter may bring snow to higher elevations.

Q: Are there any circular walking routes in Sierra De Baza?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6881594" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de la Taza – Narváez Viewpoint loop from El Granero&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 6.6 miles (10.7 km) trail that takes you through varied terrain, including a natural spring and a viewpoint.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see while hiking?
A: Sierra De Baza is rich in biodiversity. Hikers might spot various bird species, including eagles and vultures, as well as mammals like wild boar, deer, and ibex. Keep an eye out for smaller creatures and diverse plant life, especially in the more secluded areas of the park.

Q: Are there any notable viewpoints or natural monuments to visit?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3251692" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Narváez Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offers stunning panoramic vistas. Other significant natural features include the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4487639" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Señora Pine&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3251662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain of Health&lt;/a&gt;. For those seeking higher ground, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4487641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Barbara&lt;/a&gt; summit is a prominent landmark.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Sierra De Baza?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from dense pine forests to rugged ravines, and the sense of tranquility found within the natural park.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers looking for a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981930459/barranco-de-la-fonfria-and-pino-de-la-senora-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de la Fonfría and Pino de la Señora Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 11.3 miles (18.2 km) path that navigates through a ravine and extensive pine forests, showcasing the park's rugged beauty.

Q: Where can I find parking or trailheads for the hikes?
A: Many trails in Sierra De Baza have designated parking areas near their starting points, often close to visitor centers or picnic areas within the natural park. Specific trailhead information and parking details are usually available on individual route pages on komoot, such as for the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19348150" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calar de Santa Bárbara Summit loop from Collado de la Mina&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there places to stay or facilities within the park?
A: Yes, there are several facilities available. You can find huts and shelters like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4487636" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Canaleja Alta Refuge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3403778" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de las Palomas Cyclists' Shelter&lt;/a&gt;. There's also the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430161" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Fraguara Campsite&lt;/a&gt; for those looking to stay overnight.

Q: Can I reach Sierra De Baza by public transport?
A: While public transport options directly into the heart of Sierra De Baza Natural Park are limited, you can typically reach nearby towns like Baza by bus. From there, you would likely need to arrange a taxi or have private transport to access specific trailheads within the park.
tips:
La Fuente de la Salud is a charming place located in Baza. Legend has it that this was the place where Alfonso XI set up his camp.
Its origin was due to the need to channel the spring that supplies the population.
The Health Fountain was built by Francisco del Castillo in the 16th century, although local stonemason Alonso González Bailén also took part.
It is located about 2 km northeast of Baza along the Virgen de la Salud path.
If you are a lover of nature and history, the Fountain of Health and its surroundings are a perfect place to explore. Enjoy the beauty of Baza and its landscapes! 🌿🏞️

The mountain route leaves from the Cortijo Narváez Visitors Center car park, with the possibility of visiting the facilities and discovering the historical, cultural and natural values of the natural park. A room is dedicated to popular architecture and the uses and customs of the inhabitants of the mountains.

The Mirador de Narváez is an impressive place in the Sierra de Baza Natural Park. From the top, we can see the Hoya de Baza behind and the Morrón Alto forest surveillance booth. The Calar de Santa Bárbara, at 2271 meters, is the highest peak in the Sierra de Baza Natural Park.

faqs.question: Where does the Parque Natural de Sierra de Baza – circular to Mirador de Narváez trail start, and is there parking?
faqs.answer: The trail conveniently starts near the Cortijo Narváez Visitor Center. You'll find parking available there, making it easy to begin your hike.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families and people with reduced mobility?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is very family-friendly and suitable for various fitness levels. A significant portion, known as the Sendero de Accesibilidad Universal de Narváez, is specifically designed to be accessible for people with reduced mobility and is suitable for wheelchairs, making it an inclusive option for many.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but autumn is particularly special for hearing the bellowing of stags. Spring offers vibrant flora, while the verdant landscape provides a pleasant contrast to surrounding arid plains in other seasons. Always check local weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Parque Natural de Sierra de Baza – circular to Mirador de Narváez trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally welcome on this trail. However, as it's within a Natural Park, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay an entrance fee to hike in Sierra de Baza Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a specific permit to hike this trail, nor is there an entrance fee for the Sierra de Baza Natural Park. However, always respect the park regulations to preserve its natural beauty.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hike?
faqs.answer: The path is primarily made up of easily accessible, unpaved, and natural paths. You'll walk along forest tracks and sections that were once firebreaks, winding through pine, cypress, and cedar forests. The trails are well-marked and comfortable.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll traverse attractive landscapes of dense pine forests. Highlights include the Fountain of Health and the stunning panoramic views from the Narváez Viewpoint. You'll also cross a wooden bridge over the Narváez or Casimiro ravine.

faqs.question: What wildlife and plants might I encounter?
faqs.answer: The area has a high population of deer, especially noticeable in autumn. You might also spot diverse flora like holm oaks, gall oaks, junipers, and aromatic plants such as thyme and rosemary. Birdsong is common, with jays often seen in the ravines. Keep an eye out for mistletoe on pine trees.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other trails, including the Mirador de Narváez trail itself, the Andalucía - South Route (Provincia de Granada), and the Sendero Universal de Baza. You'll also find parts of the Cortijo de Casimiro and Sendero Fuente de la Taza.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail starts conveniently near the Cortijo Narváez Visitor Center. This center provides information about the park and features an exhibition on the human history of Sierra de Baza.

faqs.question: What views can I expect from the Mirador de Narváez?
faqs.answer: From the Narváez Viewpoint, you'll enjoy extensive panoramic views of the Sierra de Baza Natural Park, including the Hoya de Baza and the park's highest peak, Calar de Santa Bárbara (2,271m). Informative panels at the viewpoint explain the visible landscapes.
